CoChat

used KiloClaw to build CoChat

•3 reviews

KiloClaw made OpenClaw easy to deploy for teams. Instead of everyone self-hosting (and debugging servers), KiloClaw gives a managed path that connects fast and runs reliably. It inspired us to connect various agents. That let us bring “OpenClaw for teams” to life inside CoChat.

#### Alternatives Considered

Report

 244 views

Agentcard

used KiloClaw to build Agentcard

•18 reviews [...] Report

 63 views

•1 review

#### What's great

I've tried running OpenClaw myself and it's kind of a nightmare. You get it working, feel great about it, then wake up the next morning and it's just... dead. KiloClaw fixes the actual annoying part. Click a button, agent is running in under a minute, and it stays running. The fact that it's built on the same infrastructure powering 1.5M+ Kilo Code users means it's not some fly-by-night hosting wrapper. 500+ models, zero markup on tokens, and if you already use Kilo Code your account and credits just carry over. Genuinely impressed.

#### What needs improvement

results.title: OpenAI wants your launches
results.content:
🔥 Our Take: Visa and Mastercard are both teaching agents to spend, top down, with specs and pilot programs. Agentcard shipped the bottom-up version: a card scoped to one purchase, dead the second it clears, so your agent can only ever spend exactly what you approved. Nobody is worried their agent can't check out. Everyone is worried about what else it might check out. One commenter watched their assistant buy a domain end to end and called it "kind of wild." The wild part wears off. The leash is what you're paying for.

## Grow your app with Setapp: revenue, users, & AI

Newsletter Sponsor

You shipped the app. Now comes the part nobody warns you about. [...] So we teamed up with OpenAI to put credits behind it. Build something with Sol, Terra, or Luna, schedule your launch for Thursday, July 23, and the top products walk away with $10k in OpenAI credits.

If you're going to kick the tires anyway, might as well get paid for it. You've got nine days: schedule your launch for July 23.

## Cards that die on purpose

Agentcard gives your AI agent its own debit card: fund a wallet, set a budget, and it issues single-use cards that close themselves the moment a charge clears. Karen Serfaty (@keyserfaty) was running Atlas when Remote acquired it, then turned down a job at Stripe to build this instead.

results.title: Agentcard: debit cards for ai agents
results.content:
14

137

Younes Alturkey 🐟

#### What's great

The single-use, charge-on-spend model is a smart take on the trust problem with agent payments — scoping each card to one purchase neatly caps the blast radius if an agent misbehaves. Also a good call making it MCP-compatible out of the gate; that's the right bet for where agent tooling is heading. Congrats on the launch! 🚀 [...] Agentcard
